skill.name: docusaurus-advanced
skill.description: Guides advanced Docusaurus configuration, client-exposed custom fields, presets, sidebars, theme swizzling, custom CSS, layouts, and MDX usage.
skill.summary: Use project-specific Docusaurus notes to navigate advanced configuration and documentation features.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, environment access, sensitive file, weak crypto, and combined-danger findings. Review of SKILL.md shows the command and crypto findings are markdown/code-reference false positives, with no prompt injection or malicious execution intent. The remaining low concern is documentation of a client-visible API URL derived from an environment variable in Docusaurus customFields.

low_findings.title: Client-visible environment-derived API URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md documents a Docusaurus customFields value that reads DOCUSAURUS_API_URL and falls back to http://127.0.0.1:8000. This is not malicious code execution, but customFields are exposed to client-side code, so users should avoid placing secrets or internal-only endpoints in this value.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly states the API URL is exposed to the client via customFields and shows process.env.DOCUSAURUS_API_URL on line 16. The value is an API URL, not a credential, so the risk is limited.

low_findings.title: Static command execution findings are markdown false positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown inline code references and a fenced TypeScript example, not executable skill logic. No evidence found of shell execution, eval behavior, or runtime command invocation in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain markdown formatting, file paths, labels, or a TypeScript code fence. There is no executable script file or instruction to run those snippets as commands.

low_findings.title: Weak crypto and sensitive file findings are false positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings do not correspond to any cryptographic operation in SKILL.md. The sensitive environment file finding is also unsupported because line 16 reads a named environment variable in an example, not a .env file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain description text, an environment variable example, and a CSS file reference. No hashing, encryption, .env file reads, or credential collection behavior appears in the file.

low_findings.title: Combined dangerous behavior heuristic not confirmed
low_findings.description: The static critical heuristic combined command execution, network, and credential-access signals. Review found no prompt injection, data exfiltration, runtime network request, credential collection, or executable command behavior in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only semantically relevant lines document a client-visible API URL example. The command execution and credential-access inputs to the heuristic are not confirmed by the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Advanced Docusaurus projects often need coordinated config, plugins, theme overrides, and MDX guidance. This skill points Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to the relevant project paths and review areas.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the Docusaurus config file path used by the project.
Explains the client-exposed API URL customFields pattern.
Highlights the classic preset, sidebar configuration, and MDX support.
Points to theme swizzling and custom navbar item locations.
References custom CSS and Layout usage for standalone React pages.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts, generated files, or full project templates.
Does not validate the current Docusaurus version or installed plugin list.
Assumes the project uses the documented textbook directory structure.
Does not provide deployment, hosting, or CI configuration guidance.

use_cases.title: Audit Docusaurus configuration
use_cases.description: Review config, custom fields, presets, and sidebars before changing a documentation site.

use_cases.title: Extend site navigation and theme behavior
use_cases.description: Find the likely files for custom navbar items, CSS overrides, and layout wrappers.

use_cases.title: Plan richer documentation pages
use_cases.description: Use MDX and React page guidance when planning interactive documentation experiences.

prompt_templates.title: Locate Docusaurus settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docusaurus-advanced skill to identify the main Docusaurus config, sidebar file, and theme-related paths for this project.

prompt_templates.title: Review customFields usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docusaurus-advanced skill to review how the API URL is exposed through customFields and list any safe configuration recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Plan theme changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docusaurus-advanced skill to plan navbar, custom CSS, and Layout changes for a Docusaurus documentation page.

prompt_templates.title: Design an advanced documentation update
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docusaurus-advanced skill to produce an implementation plan for MDX components, theme overrides, sidebars, and config changes in the textbook Docusaurus site.

output_examples.input: Review the Docusaurus setup before I add a new interactive docs page.
output: The review should start with the main config file, sidebar file, and custom CSS path.
Check whether the page needs a Layout wrapper or an MDX component.
Confirm that any API URL exposed through custom fields contains no secrets.

output_examples.input: Where should I look before changing the navbar?
output: Inspect the theme navbar item location first, especially if the project has swizzled components.
Then review the Docusaurus config and custom CSS for related navigation or visual changes.

output_examples.input: Help me plan advanced docs improvements.
output: Create a short plan covering config changes, sidebar updates, MDX usage, and theme styling.
Separate content changes from theme changes so review and testing stay focused.

best_practices: Confirm client-exposed customFields values do not contain secrets or private endpoints.
Review config, sidebars, theme components, and CSS together before making navigation changes.
Use MDX for interactive documentation only when it improves the reader workflow.
anti_patterns: Do not store credentials or tokens in values exposed through Docusaurus customFields.
Do not assume swizzled theme paths exist without checking the project tree.
Do not mix broad theme refactors with unrelated documentation content edits.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps locate and reason about advanced Docusaurus configuration, theme, sidebar, custom field, and MDX areas.

faq.question: Does it run commands or modify files by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill is guidance text and does not include scripts or executable automation.

faq.question: Can it help with Docusaurus plugins?
faq.answer: Yes. It identifies the classic preset and points reviewers toward configuration areas for plugins and presets.

faq.question: Does it support MDX planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It notes that MDX can support interactive components in documentation pages.

faq.question: What should users check before exposing an API URL?
faq.answer: They should confirm the value is safe for browser clients and does not include secrets or internal-only endpoints.

faq.question: Is this skill project-specific?
faq.answer: Yes. It references a textbook Docusaurus directory structure and may need adaptation for other projects.
